title: UI Spec API contract
description: Machine-readable interface contract embedded as a `json` fenced block under the `## API` heading of an spec document. Skin-agnostic: it names the axes of variation, structural slots, events, accessibility expectations, and the design-token roles the spec consumes. Implementations are validated against it.

props.description: The configurable inputs of the spec.

slots.description: Named structural regions the spec exposes for content.

events.description: Semantic events the spec emits (implementation-agnostic names).

states.description: The interaction states the spec can be in.

tokens.description: Design-token roles this spec consumes (may use `*` wildcards).

responsive.description: How the spec adapts to available width. Patterns reference the shared vocabulary in responsive/patterns.json; breakpoints reference tokens.json `breakpoint.*` ids.

container.description: True when the spec adapts to its container's width, not just the viewport.

minTarget.description: Minimum pointer target size the spec must preserve at any width (e.g. `44px`).

reflow.description: The structural adaptations the spec makes, each a named pattern optionally bound to a breakpoint.

at.description: Breakpoint id from tokens.json `breakpoint.*` below which the pattern applies. Omit for adaptations with no fixed threshold (fluid, wrap, reposition, truncate).
